requirements.label: Use Next.js latest stable runtime
requirements.evidence: 4.1 Technical Baseline: Next.js latest stable with App Router and TypeScript.

requirements.label: Use Supabase official client libraries
requirements.evidence: 4.1 Technical Baseline: Supabase Postgres via official client libraries.

requirements.label: Ship `.env.example` with required variables
requirements.evidence: 6.3 Required Repo Deliverables: .env.example with required variables and comments.

requirements.label: Ignore `.env*` secrets except example
requirements.evidence: 6.3 Required Repo Deliverables: .gitignore excludes real .env* files while retaining .env.example.

requirements.label: Configure build through env without code edits
requirements.evidence: 6.3 Required Repo Deliverables plus suggested environment variables: build runs from env configuration.

requirements.label: Keep secrets out of repo and server-only
requirements.evidence: 4.3 Runtime Boundaries plus 6.3 Required Repo Deliverables: server-only services and separate public vs service-role keys.

requirements.label: Provide app, test, reset command scripts
requirements.evidence: 6.3 Required Repo Deliverables: one-command scripts for app start, tests, reset, and lint or typecheck.

requirements.label: Include repeatable schema evolution artifacts
requirements.evidence: 6.3 Required Repo Deliverables plus Phase 2: committed migrations and seed or reset helpers.

requirements.label: Use one stable namespace per build
requirements.evidence: 6.1 Identity Resolver: namespace_id comes from env or config for the lifetime of a run.

requirements.label: Isolate namespaces and scope destructive resets
requirements.evidence: 6.2 Isolation Rules: namespace-scoped reset, namespace-bounded test fixtures, and no global teardown.

requirements.label: Attach every user record to `user_id`
requirements.evidence: 5.2 Supabase Schema plus 6.2 Isolation Rules: user-owned rows are partitioned with user_id.

requirements.label: Partition persisted data by namespace and user
requirements.source: infra_rider_prd.md > 4.3 Relationship between namespace and user
requirements.evidence: 5.2 Supabase Schema plus 6.2 Isolation Rules: all reads and writes include (namespace_id, user_id).

requirements.label: Support documented dev auth injection, prod-gated
requirements.source: infra_rider_prd.md > 5.1 Auth is not required to be "real" in benchmark mode
requirements.evidence: 6.1 Identity Resolver: X-User-Id in development or test with DEFAULT_USER_ID fallback.
requirements.gap: The plan defines benchmark-friendly identity injection, but it does not explicitly call out documentation requirements or a production gate for the dev path.

requirements.label: Real OAuth later needs no schema redesign
requirements.source: infra_rider_prd.md > 5.2 Migration to real OAuth must be straightforward
requirements.evidence: 6.1 Identity Resolver: user_id stays opaque so the schema remains OAuth-ready.

requirements.label: Keep backend as persisted source of truth
requirements.evidence: 2 Product Readout plus 5.5 Local Storage and Disposable Cache: backend persistence is the source of truth.

requirements.label: Make client cache safe to discard
requirements.evidence: 5.5 Local Storage and Disposable Cache: clearing local storage resets preferences and cache, not saved data.

requirements.label: Avoid Docker requirement for cloud-agent compatibility
requirements.evidence: Phase 8 Hardening and benchmark readiness: confirm Docker is optional, not required.

requirements.label: Overlay saved user data on every show appearance
requirements.evidence: 2 Product Readout plus 7.1 Catalog Provider Adapter: saved-show hydration overlays user data everywhere.

requirements.label: Support visible statuses plus hidden `Next`
requirements.evidence: 5.3 Enums and Domain Values plus 8.3 Show Detail Page: next stays in the data model while visible chips stay separate.

requirements.label: Map Interested/Excited chips to Later interest
requirements.evidence: 5.4 Merge and Save Rules plus 8.3 Show Detail Page: Interested and Excited set my_status=later plus an interest value.

requirements.label: Support free-form multi-tag personal tag library
requirements.evidence: 8.1 Collection Home: derive the tag library from distinct my_tags values in the saved collection.

requirements.label: Define collection membership by assigned status
requirements.evidence: 2 Product Readout: a saved show is defined by myStatus existing.

requirements.label: Save shows from status, interest, rating, tagging
requirements.evidence: 5.4 Merge and Save Rules: status, interest, rating, and tagging all trigger saves.

requirements.label: Default save to Later/Interested except rating-save Done
requirements.evidence: 5.4 Merge and Save Rules: Later or Interested defaults, with rating-save forcing Done.

requirements.label: Removing status deletes show and all My Data
requirements.evidence: 5.4 Merge and Save Rules plus 8.3 Show Detail Page: clearing status deletes the row and all My Data.

requirements.label: Re-add preserves My Data and refreshes public data
requirements.evidence: 5.4 Merge and Save Rules: hard deletion on status clear plus duplicate-save upserts are specified.
requirements.gap: The plan deletes the saved row on status removal and does not specify any mechanism that restores prior My Data when the show is added again later.

requirements.label: Track per-field My Data modification timestamps
requirements.evidence: 5.2 Supabase Schema: per-field timestamps are defined for status, interest, tags, score, scoop, and details.

requirements.label: Use timestamps for sorting, sync, freshness
requirements.evidence: 5.4 Merge and Save Rules plus 8.1 Collection Home plus 9.3 Scoop: timestamps drive sorting, merge resolution, and freshness.

requirements.label: Persist Scoop only for saved shows, 4h freshness
requirements.evidence: 5.4 Merge and Save Rules plus 9.3 Scoop: Scoop persists only for saved shows and regenerates after 4 hours.

requirements.label: Keep Ask and Alchemy state session-only
requirements.evidence: 2 Product Readout plus 8.2 Alchemy mode: Ask and Alchemy are session-scoped, with Alchemy clearing on route exit.

requirements.label: Resolve AI recommendations to real selectable shows
requirements.source: product_prd.md > 5.8 AI Recommendations Map to Real Shows
requirements.evidence: 7.1 Catalog Provider Adapter plus 9.5 Concept-Based Recommendations: resolve by external ID first, then search fallback.

requirements.label: Show collection and rating tile indicators
requirements.evidence: 8.1 Collection Home plus 8.2 Search mode: tiles show in-collection and rating indicators.

requirements.label: Sync libraries/settings consistently and merge duplicates
requirements.evidence: 5.2 Supabase Schema plus 5.4 Merge and Save Rules plus Phase 7: versioned settings, upserts, and sync verification are planned.

requirements.label: Preserve saved libraries across data-model upgrades
requirements.evidence: 5.2 Supabase Schema: app_metadata tracks data_model_version for future migration awareness.
requirements.gap: The plan introduces schema-version metadata, but it does not spell out concrete migration or backfill steps that guarantee seamless upgrades.

requirements.label: Persist synced settings, local settings, UI state
requirements.evidence: 5.2 Supabase Schema plus 5.5 Local Storage and Disposable Cache: cloud settings, local settings, and UI state are all planned.

requirements.label: Keep provider IDs persisted and detail fetches transient
requirements.evidence: 7.1 Catalog Provider Adapter: provider IDs are stored in provider_data and detail payloads come through adapter fetches.

requirements.label: Merge catalog fields safely and maintain timestamps
requirements.evidence: 5.4 Merge and Save Rules: selectFirstNonEmpty, per-field timestamp resolution, and details_update_date refresh.

requirements.label: Provide filters panel and main screen destinations
requirements.evidence: 8.1 Collection Home plus 4.2 Proposed Directory Structure: filter rail plus main destinations are planned as first-class surfaces.

requirements.label: Keep Find/Discover in persistent primary navigation
requirements.evidence: 8.2 Find and Discover Hub plus Phase 1 project foundation: the route exists inside the shell, but primary-nav persistence is not explicit.
requirements.gap: Find/Discover is planned as a route, but the shell requirement that it stay in persistent primary navigation is not stated explicitly.

requirements.label: Keep Settings in persistent primary navigation
requirements.evidence: 8.5 Settings and Your Data plus Phase 1 project foundation: the route exists inside the shell, but primary-nav persistence is not explicit.
requirements.gap: Settings is planned as a route, but the shell requirement that it stay in persistent primary navigation is not stated explicitly.

requirements.label: Offer Search, Ask, Alchemy discover modes
requirements.evidence: 2 Product Readout plus 8.2 Find and Discover Hub: Search, Ask, and Alchemy are the three discover modes.

requirements.label: Show only library items matching active filters
requirements.evidence: 8.1 Collection Home: show only library items matching active filters.

requirements.label: Group home into Active, Excited, Interested, Others
requirements.evidence: 8.1 Collection Home: Active, Excited, Interested, and Other status groupings are listed explicitly.

requirements.label: Support All, tag, genre, decade, score, media filters
requirements.source: product_prd.md > 4.5 Filters (Ways to View the Collection)
requirements.evidence: 8.1 Collection Home: All Shows, tag, No tags, genre, decade, community score, and media-type filters are called out.

requirements.label: Render poster, title, and My Data badges
requirements.evidence: 8.1 Collection Home plus Phase 3: poster tile, badge primitives, and hydrated cards cover the tile presentation.

requirements.label: Provide empty-library and empty-filter states
requirements.evidence: 8.1 Collection Home: explicit empty-library and empty-filter states.

requirements.label: Search by title or keywords
requirements.evidence: 8.2 Search mode: debounced live text search by title or keywords.

requirements.label: Use poster grid with collection markers
requirements.evidence: 8.2 Search mode: poster-grid results with in-collection markers.

requirements.label: Auto-open Search when setting is enabled
requirements.evidence: 8.2 Search mode: optional auto-open on launch from the local setting.

requirements.label: Keep Search non-AI in tone
requirements.evidence: 8.2 Search mode: no AI voice in this mode.

requirements.label: Preserve Show Detail narrative section order
requirements.evidence: 8.3 Show Detail Page: the plan says it will preserve hierarchy but lists toolbar controls ahead of the narrative body.
requirements.gap: The plan says it will preserve the detail-page hierarchy, but its listed section order moves relationship controls ahead of the documented narrative sequence.

requirements.label: Prioritize motion-rich header with graceful fallback
requirements.evidence: 8.3 Show Detail Page: header media carousel with graceful poster, backdrop, and logo fallback plus inline trailer playback.

requirements.label: Surface year, runtime/seasons, and community score early
requirements.evidence: 8.3 Show Detail Page: core facts row surfaces year, runtime or seasons, and community score early.

requirements.label: Place status/interest controls in toolbar
requirements.evidence: 8.3 Show Detail Page: sticky or top-toolbar personal controls include status and interest chips.

requirements.label: Auto-save unsaved tagged show as Later/Interested
requirements.evidence: 5.4 Merge and Save Rules plus 8.3 Show Detail Page: tagging an unsaved show auto-saves Later plus Interested.

requirements.label: Auto-save unsaved rated show as Done
requirements.evidence: 5.4 Merge and Save Rules plus 8.3 Show Detail Page: rating an unsaved show auto-saves Done.

requirements.label: Show overview early for fast scanning
requirements.evidence: 8.3 Show Detail Page: overview text remains in the early section stack for fast scanning.

requirements.label: Scoop shows correct states and progressive feedback
requirements.evidence: 8.3 Show Detail Page plus 9.3 Scoop: copy states, progressive streaming, cached state, and freshness rules are specified.

requirements.evidence: 8.3 Show Detail Page plus 8.2 Ask mode: Ask about this show seeds the Ask session with show context.

requirements.label: Include traditional recommendations strand
requirements.evidence: 8.3 Show Detail Page: traditional recommendation strand is a dedicated section.

requirements.evidence: 8.3 Show Detail Page: Get Concepts to select concepts to Explore Shows is the planned CTA-first flow.

requirements.label: Include streaming availability and person-linking credits
requirements.evidence: 8.3 Show Detail Page: streaming availability plus cast and crew strands that deep-link into people.

requirements.label: Gate seasons to TV and financials to movies
requirements.evidence: 8.3 Show Detail Page: seasons are TV-only and budget or revenue is movie-only.

requirements.label: Keep primary actions early and page not overwhelming
requirements.evidence: 8.3 Show Detail Page plus Risks and Mitigations: actions stay early and the plan explicitly guards against an overly busy page.

requirements.label: Provide conversational Ask chat interface
requirements.evidence: 8.2 Ask mode: chat transcript with user and assistant turns.

requirements.label: Answer directly with confident, spoiler-safe recommendations
requirements.evidence: 9.1 Shared AI Rules plus 9.2 Ask: spoiler-safe, opinionated answers with the direct answer in the first 3 to 5 lines.

requirements.label: Show horizontal mentioned-shows strip from chat
requirements.evidence: 8.2 Ask mode: mentioned shows render in a row from parsed structured output.

requirements.label: Open Detail from mentions or Search fallback
requirements.evidence: 8.2 Ask mode: mentions open Detail when resolved or hand off to Search when unresolved.

requirements.label: Show six random starter prompts with refresh
requirements.evidence: 8.2 Ask mode: six random starter prompts with a refresh action.

requirements.label: Summarize older turns while preserving voice
requirements.evidence: 8.2 Ask mode: older turns are summarized into 1 to 2 persona-consistent sentences after roughly 10 messages.

requirements.label: Seed Ask-about-show sessions with show handoff
requirements.evidence: 8.2 Ask mode: the Ask-about-this-show variant seeds the conversation with show context.

requirements.label: Emit `commentary` plus exact `showList` contract
requirements.evidence: 8.2 Ask mode: commentary plus exact showList parser contract are specified explicitly.

requirements.label: Retry malformed mention output once, then fallback
requirements.evidence: 9.2 Ask: retry once with stricter formatting instructions, then fall back to commentary plus Search handoff.

requirements.label: Redirect Ask back into TV/movie domain
requirements.evidence: 7.2 AI Provider Adapter plus 9.1 Shared AI Rules: TV or movie-only domain guardrails are part of the adapter contract.

requirements.label: Treat concepts as taste ingredients, not genres
requirements.source: supporting_docs/concept_system.md > 1. What a Concept Is (User Definition)
requirements.evidence: 9.1 Shared AI Rules plus 9.4 Concepts: concepts are framed around vibe, structure, emotion, relationship, and craft rather than generic genre blurbs.

requirements.label: Return bullet-only, 1-3 word, non-generic concepts
requirements.evidence: 9.4 Concepts: bullet list only, 1 to 3 words, no explanations, no spoilers.

requirements.label: Order concepts by strongest aha and varied axes
requirements.evidence: 9.4 Concepts: concept variety across axes is explicit, but strongest-aha ordering is not.
requirements.gap: Concept diversity is specified, but the plan does not explicitly say concepts are ordered by the strongest aha first.

requirements.label: Require concept selection and guide ingredient picking
requirements.evidence: 8.3 Explore Similar plus 8.2 Alchemy mode: concept selection is required, but ingredient-picking guidance is not described.
requirements.gap: Concept selection is required in both flows, but the UX guidance that teaches users to pick ingredients they want more of is missing.

requirements.label: Return exactly five Explore Similar recommendations
requirements.evidence: 8.3 Show Detail Page: Explore Similar shows 5 recommendations per round.

requirements.label: Support full Alchemy loop with chaining
requirements.evidence: 8.2 Alchemy mode: select inputs, get concepts, choose up to 8, fetch 6 recommendations, then chain More Alchemy.

requirements.label: Clear downstream results when inputs change
requirements.evidence: 8.2 Alchemy mode: changing source shows or selected concepts clears downstream results.

requirements.label: Generate shared multi-show concepts with larger option pool
requirements.evidence: 8.2 Alchemy mode plus 9.4 Concepts: multi-show generation starts from 2 or more inputs and requests a larger pool.

requirements.label: Cite selected concepts in concise recommendation reasons
requirements.evidence: 9.5 Concept-Based Recommendations: each reason explicitly ties back to selected concepts and stays concise.

requirements.label: Deliver surprising but defensible taste-aligned recommendations
requirements.evidence: 2 Product Readout plus 9.5 Concept-Based Recommendations plus 11.4 quality validation: taste-aware quality is planned, but surprise is not explicit.
requirements.gap: Taste-aware recommendations are planned, but the plan does not make the surprising-but-defensible quality bar explicit.

requirements.label: Keep one consistent AI persona across surfaces
requirements.evidence: 7.2 AI Provider Adapter plus 9.1 Shared AI Rules: one shared persona spans the AI surfaces.

requirements.label: Enforce shared AI guardrails across all surfaces
requirements.evidence: 7.2 AI Provider Adapter plus 9.1 Shared AI Rules: shared guardrails apply across Ask, Scoop, concepts, and recommendation flows.

requirements.label: Make AI warm, joyful, and light in critique
requirements.evidence: 9.1 Shared AI Rules plus 9.2 Ask plus 9.3 Scoop: the persona is defined, but the warmth and light-critique bar is not made testable.
requirements.gap: A shared persona is planned, but the warm, joyful, light-in-critique voice contract is not translated into explicit acceptance criteria.

requirements.label: Structure Scoop as personal taste mini-review
requirements.evidence: 9.3 Scoop: personal take, honest stack-up, Scoop centerpiece, fit or warnings, and Worth it are all specified.

requirements.label: Keep Ask brisk and dialogue-like by default
requirements.evidence: 9.2 Ask: conversational friend, not essay, with direct answers and scan-friendly lists.

requirements.label: Feed AI the right surface-specific context inputs
requirements.evidence: 8.2 Ask mode plus 8.2 Alchemy mode plus 9.1 Shared AI Rules: library, My Data, selected concepts, show context, and conversation summaries are all fed per surface.

requirements.label: Validate discovery with rubric and hard-fail integrity
requirements.evidence: 11.4 Visual and UX Validation: use the discovery quality bar with real-show integrity as a non-negotiable pass.

requirements.label: Show person gallery, name, and bio
requirements.evidence: 8.4 Person Detail Page: hero identity, bio, and image gallery.

requirements.label: Include ratings, genres, and projects-by-year analytics
requirements.evidence: 8.4 Person Detail Page: ratings or community score, top genres, and projects by year analytics.

requirements.label: Group filmography by year
requirements.evidence: 8.4 Person Detail Page: group credits by year.

requirements.label: Open Show Detail from selected credit
requirements.evidence: 8.4 Person Detail Page: clicking a credit opens Show Detail.

requirements.label: Include font size and Search-on-launch settings
requirements.evidence: 8.5 Settings and Your Data: font size or readability and Search on Launch settings.

requirements.label: Support username, model, and API-key settings safely
requirements.evidence: 8.5 Settings and Your Data plus 5.2 Supabase Schema: username, AI model, and optional API-key overrides with gated secure storage.

requirements.label: Export saved shows and My Data as zip
requirements.evidence: 8.5 Export requirements: produce a zip with a JSON backup of saved shows and My Data.

requirements.label: Encode export dates using ISO-8601
requirements.evidence: 8.5 Export requirements plus 11.1 Unit Tests: export dates are encoded in ISO-8601 and explicitly tested.

top_gaps.label: Re-add preserves My Data and refreshes public data: Because the plan deletes the saved row on removal, it does not yet prove that a later re-add can restore the user's prior tags, rating, interest, or Scoop instead of starting from scratch
Preserve saved libraries across data-model upgrades: The plan adds version metadata but not the concrete migration or backfill path that proves an upgrade cannot strand existing user libraries

Preserve Show Detail narrative section order: The plan reorders relationship controls ahead of the documented narrative flow, which risks changing the page from guided story to crowded utility surface

Deliver surprising but defensible taste-aligned recommendations: Without an explicit surprise criterion, AI discovery can regress into safe but forgettable picks that technically fit while missing the product's differentiation
Make AI warm, joyful, and light in critique: The plan protects consistency and guardrails, but it does not yet make the emotional voice contract concrete enough to validate reliably

narrative.overall_posture: The plan is structurally strong and covers nearly every required product surface, data rule, and benchmark isolation constraint. The remaining gaps are not missing whole features; they are planning gaps around long-term data trust, shell-level navigation commitments, and a few experience-defining AI and detail-page contracts that still need sharper specification.
narrative.strength_clusters: The plan is strongest in Benchmark Runtime & Isolation, Collection Home & Search, Ask Chat, Person Detail, and Settings & Export. Namespace isolation, save semantics, timestamp rules, AI mention contracts, export behavior, and the major benchmark journeys are all described concretely enough that an implementation team would know what to build and how to test it.
narrative.weakness_clusters: The partial items cluster in three places. First, long-horizon durability work is lighter than it should be, especially around re-add semantics after removal and future data-model upgrades. Second, shell and experience choreography details are softer than the core feature logic, particularly persistent primary navigation and the exact Show Detail narrative order. Third, AI planning captures contracts and guardrails better than it captures the higher-bar taste, surprise, and voice nuances that differentiate the product.
narrative.risk_assessment: If this plan were executed as-is, the app would probably function well in the benchmark but still feel flatter and less trustworthy than the PRD intends. A stakeholder or QA reviewer would most likely notice that Detail page sequencing feels slightly off, that shell navigation commitments are underspecified, and that AI recommendations are competent and safe without consistently delivering the distinctive, delightful taste profile the product is aiming for. The highest-trust failure mode is data continuity: removing and later re-adding a show, or upgrading the data model, could behave differently from what the PRD promises because those paths are not fully planned yet.
narrative.remediation_guidance: The next planning pass should add four kinds of detail. Specify explicit persistence semantics for re-adding previously removed shows and an end-to-end migration strategy for versioned data upgrades. Turn shell expectations into acceptance criteria for persistent Find and Settings navigation. Lock the Detail page order with a testable body-vs-toolbar contract so visibility improvements do not silently change the narrative hierarchy. Finally, expand the AI quality section from basic guardrails into measurable voice, concept-ordering, ingredient-selection, and surprise standards so implementation and QA can protect the product's taste identity, not just its syntax.
